projectDescription: A minimal, complete Pokémon battle simulation engine written in Zig with TypeScript/JavaScript bindings, optimized for performance and designed for AI, tooling, and embedded use cases.
keyFeatures: 1000x faster than Pokémon Showdown in compatibility mode
Frame-accurate and bug-for-bug compatible with Gen I & II cartridges
Pokémon Showdown compatibility mode via -Dshowdown build flag
Binary wire protocol with optional logging via -Dlog build flag
Supports Zig, TypeScript/JavaScript, C, and WebAssembly targets
Zero dynamic memory allocation — fixed-size pre-allocated buffers
Deterministic RNG control with FixedRNG for AI/testing use cases
Data-oriented design with cache-friendly packed data structures
Probability tracking and damage calc support via optional build flags
publicApiSurface: Battle.create(gen, options) — initialize a new battle
Battle.restore(gen, battle, options) — restore an in-progress battle
battle.update(c1, c2) — advance battle state with player choices
battle.choices(player, result) — enumerate valid choices for a player
battle.log — binary protocol log DataView from last update
Log.parse(line, info) — parse binary protocol into Pokémon Showdown format
initialize(options) — set up the native engine addon
Lookup.get(gen) — get generation-specific data lookup table
Result — battle update outcome with type and player flags
pkmn.gen1.Battle — Zig Generation I battle type
pkmn.gen2.Battle — Zig Generation II battle type
